div1.entryFree: Heap, vb. to pile, to accumulate, to lay up a great quantity of: with measure --ed, As V, 4, 185 (overfull, more than brimful). if the measure of thy joy be --ed like mine, Rom. II, 6, 25. the --ing friendships, Wint. IV, 2, 22 (receiving plenty of good services). mountainous error would be too highly --ed, Cor. II, 3, 127. the late dignities --ed up to them, Mcb. I, 6, 19 (cf. To). Followed by on, == to load, to bestow plenteously: Gent. III, 1, 19. H6B II, 1, 187. H6C II, 2, 158. R3 III, 7, 204. H8 III, 2, 175. IV, 2, 64. Tim. IV, 1, 22. Caes. I, 2, 134. Cymb. I, 1, 132.
